migration_policy: additive until 1.x; breaking changes require migration note, fixture update, backwards-compatibility decision, explicit reviewer approval
title: CC item variant
description: A semantically-equivalent variant of a practice item, generated to re-test a MISSED concept without reusing the exact stem (PR-10.4a). The variant preserves what the source item tests (objective, bank, fact_ids, expected_keywords, misconception target) and changes only surface wording. It carries NO answer-key field: correctness lives in a SEPARATE, evaluator-context variant key (correct_index), exactly like answer-keys.json for gold items, so no correct index, display letter, or choice order is ever stored here. The semantic_lock does NOT define correctness on its own; it CORROBORATES the key — the keyed-correct choice must contain every must_include_keyword and no other choice may. Draft/api-generated variants are local-only and never enter readiness, holdout, or a mock draw until reviewed. additionalProperties:false (and the code's allowlist check) makes this a closed object so no answer-bearing field of any name can be smuggled in.

source_item_id.description: The practice item this variant re-tests. Must resolve in the practice goldset and must NOT be a holdout item.

objective.description: Must equal the source item's objective (no drift).

bank.description: Must equal the source item's bank (no drift).

fact_ids.description: Grounding fact cards. Must equal or be a subset of the source item's fact_ids — a variant may not introduce a new claim.

expected_keywords.description: Keywords that must be supported by the cited fact cards (unsupported_claim_guard, reused from the item validator).

misconception_ids.description: Registry misconception ids the distractors target. Each resolves in reference/misconception-registry.json and must be a subset of the source item's misconception targets (no target drift).

semantic_lock.description: The invariant contract that CORROBORATES the isolated variant key (it does not replace it). The keyed-correct choice must contain every must_include_keyword and no other choice may, so the lock keywords uniquely discriminate the correct choice; there is no stored correct index here.

must_test.description: The concept the item tests, in one sentence.

correct_concept.description: The correct answer as a concept (e.g. 'TCP'), never a display letter — the validator rejects a bare A/B/C/D/index. Descriptive; correctness is keyed by the isolated variant key.

must_include_keywords.description: Keywords that must appear in the correct choice (and only the correct choice). Must be a subset of expected_keywords so the identifying keywords are also grounded.

must_not_change.description: The source properties that must not drift (objective, bank, fact_ids, misconception_ids).

stem_variant.description: Reworded scenario/stem. Must differ meaningfully from the source stem (not a synonym swap) yet test the same concept.

choices_variant.description: The reworded choices in canonical (authoring) order. Rendered to the learner via the PR-10.3 presentation layer, which shuffles per attempt — the variant never stores a display order.

status.description: draft: unvetted (incl. api_generated_local). validated: passes validate_variant. reviewed: adversarially reviewed and practice-eligible. rejected: failed review. Only validated/reviewed with review metadata are practice-eligible; draft never enters readiness/holdout/mock.

source.description: Provenance. api_generated_local is always draft and local-only until reviewed (PR-10.4b).

reviewed_by.description: Anonymised reviewer handle; required once status is reviewed.

last_reviewed.description: ISO date of the review; required once status is reviewed.

readiness_weight.description: Variants NEVER contribute to the governed readiness signal. If present it must be 0; the engine treats variants as practice-only.
